headline.biggest_gap_line: Multi-Agent / Tool-Use Capability vs Scalable Oversight (cap +21% / saf -6%)
headline.meaning: The defining tension this quarter is autonomous agents advancing rapidly while the oversight needed to supervise them at scale is stalling — capability for multi-agent/tool-use systems grew +20.7% QoQ while scalable oversight contracted -5.6%.
headline.why_it_matters: Agents are being deployed into shared, stateful environments (repos, infrastructure) where per-agent benchmarks miss accumulated systemic risk, yet the oversight methods to catch this are losing momentum — a widening supervision gap precisely where autonomy is scaling.

overall_foresight.risk: As scalable-oversight research decelerates while agentic deployment accelerates, the safest available oversight techniques are being frozen into classified government evaluation regimes just as their methods stop maturing — locking in a static evaluation standard against fast-moving capabilities.

Chain-of-Thought Faithfulness research is accelerating right as SB 53 / RAISE-style transparency laws take effect, creating pressure to publish faithfulness metrics as compliance artifacts before the field agrees what 'faithful' means — freezing a premature standard into reporting law.

PRC influence operations are now probing the data-center/electricity-cost narrative — the same grid-strain pressure point that is becoming AI's hard physical constraint — turning energy infrastructure into a combined cyber-narrative attack surface that neither AI-safety nor grid-security communities own.

weekly_foresight.risk: Activation-steering and memorization-indicator techniques being published as interpretability/calibration tools are quietly becoming the technical substrate for *covert internal content control*, arriving the same week US frontier-model evaluations moved behind a classified curtain — meaning the public can no longer see how internal steering is being used.

LLM factual-error-detection research is shifting its training reference from human-written 'ground truth' to LLM-generated text — quietly normalizing AI output as the standard of factual correctness just as US public trust in AI accuracy collapses, creating a self-referential truth baseline that institutions will inherit unknowingly.
